_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron)
_meta.note: Newport News VA local batch — evidence-finalize Vice Mayor Bethany, councilmen Long/Eley/Harris, and (mark-lost) Commonwealth's Attorney Howard Gwynn. NN council is officially nonpartisan; Long/Bethany/Harris/Eley ran as independents but several have Dem-Committee ties. KEY ROSTER FIXES: (1) Gwynn LOST the June 17, 2025 Democratic primary 57.15%-42.85% to Shannon Jones; no Republican filed; term ended 12/31/2025; successor took office 1/1/2026. Mark status=lost / candidacy_status=lost. (2) Eley is in his FIRST council term (Jan 2023-Dec 2026), not 'long-serving' — his prior service was on NNPS school board. KEY EVIDENCE: Nov 26, 2024 NN City Council voted 6-1 to expand the firearms ban in city-owned buildings — Long voted YES with the majority (direct quote on file); Eley was the SOLE NO with three on-record self-defense quotes; Bethany sat as Vice Mayor and was in the 6 by elimination. Harris's term began 1/1/2025 so he was not yet on council for that vote. All four sitting councilmembers (Long, Bethany, Eley, Harris) voted 7-0 to adopt the FY2026 and FY2027 city budgets holding the real-estate rate at $1.18/$100 — routine governance, scored null on the lone applicable economic cell (q2/BBA-style) per the high-bar discipline used in fxbg-2026-06.json.

profile.religion: Catholic (campaign bio: family attends 'weekly Mass' together)
profile.education: B.S. General Science, Morehouse College; B.S. Mechanical Engineering, Georgia Institute of Technology; Virginia Professional Engineer (P.E.) licensure 2011
profile.background: Engineer at Zachry Nuclear; Newport News native and NNPS graduate. Civic boards: NN Board of Zoning Appeals, Virginia Living Museum Trustees, Boys & Girls Club of Virginia Peninsula, Smart Beginnings Virginia Peninsula, Warwick Rotary Club. Newport News City Council, Central District (Seat B), term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (on the Nov 2026 ballot).

cleon-long.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Newport News City Council, Central District / Seat B (nonpartisan office; ran as independent; filed Jan 2026 to seek Co-Chair of the Newport News Democratic City Committee — hard partisan affiliation despite the nonpartisan ballot line). Term ends 12/31/2026; on the Nov 2026 ballot. HARD evidence on self_defense: voted YES on the Nov 26, 2024 6-1 ordinance EXPANDING the firearms ban in all NN city-owned buildings and recreation/community centers ($500 civil penalty for open or concealed carry, including ammunition and components) — only Councilman Eley dissented. On the record, Long defended the ordinance: 'This ordinance also sets up for our public buildings to have metal detectors' and (on guns left in vehicles in city lots) 'Our parking lots are secured and monitored, so the thought that your car is going to be broken into, I think, that's not of concern.' Voted YES (7-0) on the FY2027 operating budget ($1.26B, adopted May 12, 2026) holding the real-estate tax rate FLAT at $1.18/$100 — routine governance, scored null on economic_stewardship q2 (BBA framing). Catholic — campaign bio states family attends weekly Mass together (primary source, his own campaign site). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on life, marriage/sexuality, family/schools (rubric topics), Christian liberty, election integrity, immigration (personal statement), refuse_state_overreach — null per the skill, not inferred from party.

0.note: Voted YES on Nov 26, 2024 6-1 ordinance expanding the firearms ban in all NN city-owned buildings (only Eley dissented). Defended the ordinance on record citing metal detectors and 'secured and monitored' city parking lots — a posture contrary to constitutional/permitless carry rights on public property.

1.note: Voted YES on the 11/26/2024 expansion of the city firearms ban — a local gun-regulation expansion banning open AND concealed carry plus ammunition/components in city-owned buildings, with a $500 civil penalty.

profile.education: Woodside HS (Newport News, 2009); A.A. Business Admin, Virginia Peninsula CC; B.B.A. (accounting + finance), Old Dominion University; M.A. Public Policy/Public Administration, Liberty University; business-analytics certificate, Cornell University
profile.background: Financial Analyst / Partner Accountant at Ferguson Enterprises (Newport News HQ) since Sept 2018. Former vice chair, NN Human Rights Commission. Chairs NN City Council Finance & Government Performance Committee. Vice Mayor of Newport News (youngest in NN history and youngest Black citizen ever elected in Newport News). VML Vice President (elected Oct 2025). Newport News City Council, North District / Seat B, term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (on the Nov 2026 ballot).

curtis-bethany.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Vice Mayor of Newport News + Council member North District / Seat B (nonpartisan office; ran as independent; opponent Maxwell also independent). Term ends 12/31/2026; on the Nov 2026 ballot. Newport News North District has TWO seats (Seat A held by Marcellus Harris III, term 2025-2028; Seat B / Vice Mayor held by Bethany, term 2023-2026) — both records in scorecard are legitimately current. HARD evidence on self_defense: as a sitting council member he was in the 6-1 majority that voted on Nov 26, 2024 to EXPAND the NN firearms ban in city-owned buildings and rec/community centers (only Eley dissented; Long quoted defending the ordinance). Voted YES (7-0) on the FY2026 and FY2027 city budgets ($1.26B and $1.267B respectively), holding the real-estate tax rate FLAT at $1.18/$100 — routine governance, scored null on the BBA-framed economic_stewardship q2. Lead public-safety posture is community-based violence prevention (Momentum for Peace, gun-violence-prevention grants for mentoring/counseling/conflict-resolution), driven in part by a brother killed by gun violence — a substantive but community-led posture, NOT affirmative public backing of NNPD that the public_justice q0 high bar requires; scored null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on life, marriage/sexuality (no LGBTQ Victory Fund / Equality Virginia endorsement found; the June 2025 NN Pride proclamation was issued by Mayor Jones, not a council vote), family/schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, immigration (personal statement), refuse_state_overreach (his only state-relationship rhetoric is collaborative VML Vice President statements). No public church/faith affiliation found.

0.note: As a sitting council member, in the 6-1 majority on the Nov 26, 2024 ordinance expanding the NN firearms ban in all city-owned buildings and rec/community centers (only Eley dissented). Posture contrary to permitless/constitutional carry rights on public property.

1.note: In the 6-1 majority on the 11/26/2024 expansion of the city firearms ban — a local gun-regulation expansion banning open AND concealed carry plus ammunition/components in city-owned buildings, $500 civil penalty.

profile.education: Carver HS valedictorian (segregated); B.A. Dartmouth College; J.D. University of Michigan Law School
profile.background: Joined Newport News Commonwealth's Attorney's office May 1, 1982; elected Commonwealth's Attorney via special election 1990; eight consecutive unopposed re-elections through 2021; second-longest-serving CA in Virginia. Lost the June 17, 2025 Democratic primary to Shannon M. Jones 57.15%-42.85%; no Republican filed; Jones unopposed in Nov 2025 general and took office 1/1/2026. Term ended 12/31/2025.

howard-gwynn.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement — STATUS UPDATE] Howard E. Gwynn lost the June 17, 2025 Democratic primary 57.15%-42.85% to Shannon M. Jones (former Asst. Commonwealth's Attorney). No Republican filed for November 2025; Jones was elected unopposed in the Nov 2025 general and took office Jan 1, 2026. Gwynn's term as Newport News Commonwealth's Attorney ENDED 12/31/2025 — as of this refinement (2026-06) he has been OUT OF OFFICE for ~5 months. Marked status=lost / candidacy_status=lost per the refine-scorecard skill (lost-primary rule). HARD evidence (preserved for the historical record): founding-era member of Virginia Progressive Prosecutors for Justice (VPPFJ), the alliance whose formal platform demands ending cash bail, abolishing the death penalty, ending mandatory minimums, ending the three-strikes felony enhancement for petty larceny, and expanding expungement (Charlottesville.gov letter to VA General Assembly, Mar 8, 2021). Progressive Voters Guide 2021 attributed to him support for 'ending three-strikes felony enhancement for petty larceny offenses, and ending cash bail' (public_justice q1 FALSE). Tempering evidence: Virginia Police Benevolent Association (NN chapter) endorsed him in the 2025 primary (rank-and-file police union endorsement — meaningful but third-party-on-him, not him publicly backing law enforcement; per the skill's high bar, public_justice q0 left null). Did not declare a marijuana-non-prosecution policy like Morales / Descano / Dehghani-Tafti. Highest-profile decision: declined to charge the 6-year-old Richneck Elementary shooter (Jan 2023; 'a 6-year-old cannot form the requisite criminal intent') and charged the assistant principal with felony child neglect. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on life, marriage/sexuality, family/schools, Christian liberty, immigration/ICE, self-defense / 2A (charging policies on those topics not located), refuse_state_overreach (no declared non-prosecution policy), church/faith affiliation. Successor Shannon Jones is NOT yet in scorecard — flag for separate add.

1.note: Founding-era member of Virginia Progressive Prosecutors for Justice (VPPFJ) — alliance signatory on the formal platform of ending cash bail, abolishing the death penalty, ending mandatory minimums, ending three-strikes felony enhancement for petty larceny, and expanding expungement. Progressive Voters Guide (2021) attributed to him support for 'ending three-strikes felony enhancement for petty larceny offenses, and ending cash bail.' That positions him on the progressive-prosecutor / cashless-bail side of public_justice q1.

profile.background: Newport News native, Menchville HS graduate. Local business owner (Your Hair Boutique; marketing consultant). Director of the What's Next Foundation (workforce-training nonprofit he co-founded with Dr. Ashby Kilgore for Southeast Newport News). Hampton Roads Community Action Program board member. Prior elected service: Newport News School Board, South District (elected May 3, 2016). Newport News City Council, South District (Seat B), term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 (on the Nov 2026 ballot; campaign 'Eley Forward' is live).

john-eley.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Newport News City Council, South District / Seat B (nonpartisan office; ran as independent; appears on Newport News Democratic City Committee endorsement-context lists). Term ends 12/31/2026; FILED for re-election (Eley Forward at johneley.com). NOTE: Eley is in his FIRST council term — prior elected service was on NNPS school board 2016+, not council. SIGNATURE HARD EVIDENCE — pro-2A: on Nov 26, 2024 Eley was the SOLE 1 in a 6-1 dissent against the council ordinance expanding the firearms ban in NN city-owned buildings. Three on-record self-defense quotes: 'I do believe people should have the right to carry their gun where they want to carry their gun. I want to be able to defend myself' (WAVY); 'This is a free country, this is America. I want to be able to, if someone comes to me and attacks me, to defend myself and shoot him' (WHRO); 'If you cause harm against me or my family, I want to be able to defend myself' (Yahoo reprint). This is a HARD pro-2A position from a Democrat-context council member — meaningful and counter-archetype. Voted YES (7-0) on the FY2026 and FY2027 city budgets ($1.26B and $1.267B), real-estate tax rate held FLAT at $1.18/$100 — routine governance, scored null on the BBA-framed economic_stewardship q2. Community work: founded/runs the iCount Program, annual Holiday Feeding & Fellowship with Olympic medalist Francena McCorory, anti-violence programming — substantive community work but not affirmative public-backing of NNPD per the skill's high bar (public_justice q0 null). NO EVIDENCE FOUND on life, marriage/sexuality (no Pride-vote individual statement; the 2025 NN Pride proclamation was the Mayor's, not a council vote), family/schools (no recovered position on CRT/parental notification/library), Christian liberty (he attended a 2022 candidate forum at First Baptist Denbigh — venue only), election integrity, immigration, refuse_state_overreach. No public church/faith affiliation found.

0.note: Sole NO vote on the 11/26/2024 6-1 ordinance expanding the firearms ban in NN city-owned buildings — defended the right to carry for self-defense on the record: 'I do believe people should have the right to carry their gun where they want to carry their gun. I want to be able to defend myself.' Affirms the spirit of permitless/constitutional carry at the local level.

1.note: Sole 6-1 dissent against a local firearms-ban expansion — actively opposed expanding restrictions on lawful carry / ammunition / components in city-owned buildings.

profile.religion: D.Div. (Canaan Theological Seminary & College, Hampton VA, 2019); former youth minister (~6 yrs) at First Baptist Church Morrison, Newport News, under his late father Rev. Dr. Marcellus L. Harris Jr. (d. Feb 10, 2024 at 81, pastor at FBC Morrison 1971-2016). Not currently a sitting pastor.
profile.education: B.S. Child Development & Family Relations, East Carolina University; M.A. Counseling, Hampton University; D.Div., Canaan Theological Seminary & College (2019)
profile.background: Professional school counselor with Newport News Public Schools. Founder, P.R.I.D.E. Program (mentoring for elementary-age boys). Youth basketball coach. Serves on the Transportation District Commission of Hampton Roads; past Hampton Roads Regional Jail Authority. Wife Rasheena Harris, M.Ed., Executive Director of Foster Care, James Barry-Robinson Institute. Newport News City Council, North District / Seat A, term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 (re-elected unopposed Nov 5, 2024; NOT on the 2026 ballot).

marcellus-harris.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Newport News City Council, North District / Seat A (nonpartisan office; ran unopposed Nov 2024). Term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on the 2026 ballot. CLARIFICATION: NN North District has TWO seats — Harris (Seat A, 2025-2028) and Vice Mayor Bethany (Seat B, 2023-2026) — both currently sitting members; the scorecard's North District double-listing is correct, not a duplicate. Harris was NOT yet on council for the Nov 26, 2024 gun-ban vote (term began 1/1/2025). HARD evidence (limited): voted YES (7-0) on the FY2026 ($1.206B, May 13, 2025) and FY2027 ($1.267B, May 12, 2026) city budgets, holding the real-estate tax rate FLAT at $1.18/$100 — routine governance, scored null on the BBA-framed economic_stewardship q2. Public-safety posture is community-based violence reduction (cites losing a student to gun violence as motivating his run; runs P.R.I.D.E. mentoring program) — not affirmative public backing of NNPD per the skill's high bar (public_justice q0 null). Holds a Doctor of Divinity; former youth minister at FBC Morrison — biographical fact (recorded in profile.religion) but NOT a documented position on any rubric cell. IMPORTANT CORRECTION (do not confuse with father): the longtime FBC Morrison pastor was his late father Rev. Dr. Marcellus L. Harris Jr. (1971-2016, d. Feb 10, 2024) — NOT the council member. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on life, marriage/sexuality (Harris not named in the May 2026 NN clergy push to revisit the Youngkin-era transgender-student policy), family/schools (specific positions), Christian liberty, election integrity, immigration, refuse_state_overreach, self-defense / 2A.
